_comment: Evidence for the shipped grc-joint-v3 weight-only quantization: the size win and the lossless-on-UD-Perseus claim in docs/benchmarks.md. The fp32 reference is grc-joint-v2 (seed replicates in seed-grc-joint-v2/summary.json); the rejected full-int8-activation recipe is in gate-report.json.
recipe: weight-only int8 (MatMulNBits, block 128, symmetric on MatMul weights) + fp16 elsewhere (including the word-embedding table); activations kept fp32
size_reduction: 173.1 MB vs 518.4 MB tar.gz (~3.0x); 182.1 MB vs 556.6 MB uncompressed onnx

ud_perseus_test_shipped_v3.note: measured through the package's own inference code fetching the grc-joint-v3 release asset (onnxruntime CPU)

lossless_gate.result: weight-only int8 + fp16 changes every UD Perseus test metric by <=0.02 points vs the fp32 grc-joint-v2 export (well within the 0.3-point gate); full int8 with quantized activations was rejected (see gate-report.json: UPOS 98.30 -> 23.34 on the gate set)
lossless_gate.reproduce: score aegean.data.fetch('grc-joint') (v3) and the fp32 grc-joint-v2 asset with greek.evaluate_on_ud('perseus','test') and diff per metric
